campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review financial reports from the transit authority covering March 2026 spending, and passed a new policy preventing the city from letting federal agencies use city buildings and property. The council also discussed ways to hire and keep city employees.
keyTopics: Transit authority financial report for March 2026
New policy restricting federal use of city property
City employee recruitment and retention efforts

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a meeting to address dangerous or neglected properties in Saratoga that the County identified. They listened to public concerns about cleaning up these hazardous sites, approved a plan to move forward with the cleanup work, and also updated the city's technology policy and established a new process to help residents who need special accommodations.
keyTopics: Public hearing on cleanup of hazardous properties
County-identified properties requiring abatement
New Technology Disruption Policy adoption
Process for requesting reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and handle several maintenance and service agreements. The council declared overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation a public nuisance and approved reports for landscape and lighting maintenance, while also authorizing the town manager to sign multi-year service contracts.
keyTopics: Hazardous vegetation (brush) declared a public nuisance - likely triggering cleanup requirements
Landscape and lighting maintenance engineer's report approved
Multiple 5-year service agreements authorized with vendors (specific vendors not fully detailed in agenda)
April 21 meeting minutes approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 6, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on May 6, 2026 to organize upcoming agenda items for two future meetings scheduled for May 12 and May 19. The council reviewed what topics to add to each meeting's agenda, set specific times for certain discussions, and handled any necessary paperwork requirements. The City Auditor also presented an activity report covering April 2026.
keyTopics: Organized agenda items for May 12, 2026 city council meeting
Organized agenda items for May 19, 2026 city council meeting
Set time schedules for specific discussion topics
City Auditor's April 2026 activity report
Reviewed documentation and notice requirements for agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on May 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate either in person or online via Zoom. Residents who wanted to speak during public comment could join the virtual meeting and use the raise hand feature to be recognized by the council chair.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom for residents
Public comment procedures for online participants
Phone dial-in option for those without internet

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, followed by a regular public meeting at 7 P.M. The agenda was light, with no specific public business items listed beyond the standard opening ceremony.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (specific topics not disclosed to public)
Small business discussion or recognition
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial review meeting about Cupertino's spending plan for fiscal year 2026-27. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city on legislation or decisions to officially register and report their lobbying activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
Rules for people attempting to influence city legislative or administrative decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 11, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting of the Economic Development & Marketing Committee on May 11, 2026. The city is continuing to offer both in-person and remote participation options so residents can attend and provide feedback on economic development and marketing initiatives.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee meeting held in-person
Remote participation available via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment submissions accepted via email by 11 AM on meeting day
Ongoing efforts to support local economic development

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve spending plans for the next several years and discuss the city's budget for 2026-27. They also adopted a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how council meetings are run.
keyTopics: Approved 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031)
Reviewed next year's operating budget proposal
Adopted new Parks and Trails Master Plan for Innovation District
Updated City Council meeting rules and procedures

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing about a proposed new apartment building at 450 Lytton Avenue that would replace a city parking lot. The project would be a 6-story building with 72 apartments, all of which would be affordable for low-income residents, and the developer is asking for special approvals to make the project possible.
keyTopics: 72 new affordable housing units on Lytton Avenue
6-story building replacing 52-space city parking lot
Request for density bonus concessions under state law
Streamlined approval process for 100% affordable housing
